div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the United Kingdom (Winant)
div.p:
4584. (1) With reference to instruction No. 1770 of September 8, 1942Not printed. you may inform Sir Frederick Leith-Ross, in response to his letters of September 3 and 4, 1942 to Mr. Acheson,The letter of September 4 here referred to was a postscript to the one of September 3, and is not printed. that we approve the following substitution for paragraphs 7 and 8 of the draft statementSee footnote 37, p. 135. to the Inter-Allied Committee in London proposed by the State Department:
“That he has been informed that while the United States Government appreciates fully the desirability of the early preparations of plans, administration, and the substance for relief,In telegram No. 4754, September 30, 9 p.m., the foregoing was corrected to read: “plans for the administration and substance of relief.” (840.50/687.) it does not believe that in the present posture of the war the time has yet come for formal discussion of an agreement looking to these ends.”
(2) Answer to your telegram No. 4712 of August 23 will be forwarded soon.

(3) Leith-Ross has suggested Dr. Penrose as chairman of a subcommittee on nutrition of the Inter-Allied Post-war Requirements Committee. Your views would be appreciated on his availability, in view of his other duties.
